desc: Graz is Austria's second city, blending a UNESCO-listed old town, striking modern architecture, and a strong food-and-design scene with lower crowd pressure than Vienna or Salzburg. Demand rises in warm months and around major events, while winter (outside festive dates) is typically the best-value period for city breaks.
hls: UNESCO-listed old town and Schlossberg viewpoints
Strong food culture in Austria's Styrian region
Blend of Renaissance courtyards and modern landmarks
Manageable crowds compared with Austria's headline cities
Easy access to South Styrian wine country

todo.name: Schlossberg and Clock Tower
todo.desc: Ride or climb to Graz's hilltop icon for city panoramas and old-town orientation.

todo.name: Old Town courtyard loop
todo.desc: Walk Hauptplatz, Landhaus courtyards, and medieval lanes through the historic center.

todo.name: Kunsthaus + Murinsel
todo.desc: Pair Graz's contemporary art museum with the floating Mur island walkway.

todo.name: Eggenberg Palace
todo.desc: Visit the baroque palace complex and gardens for one of Graz's standout cultural sites.

todo.name: Farmers' market and Styrian food day
todo.desc: Sample regional produce and Styrian specialties around Kaiser-Josef-Platz and old-town eateries.

todo.name: South Styrian wine road day trip
todo.desc: Head south for vineyard landscapes, cellar doors, and rolling-hill viewpoints.

wifi.note: Very reliable across central hotels, apartments, and coworking options, with strong mobile-data backup.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; most UK itineraries connect via Vienna, Frankfurt, or other hubs. Typical return fares are around £90-£180 in low season, £180-£280 in shoulder demand, and £280+ during summer and December holiday peaks.
prac.visa: Austria is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally visit visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Summer thunderstorms can affect afternoon sightseeing and day-trip timing.
Congress and festival dates may tighten central hotel availability quickly.
Some nearby wine-region venues have reduced opening outside peak season.

sweet: May-June and September are usually the best all-round months for weather, city atmosphere, and value before/after midsummer peaks.
